revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 右内神社 |画像 = [[File:Unai-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|display=inline,title}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=右内神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |祭神 = [[櫛石窓命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = }} '''右内神社'''（うないじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名に鎮座する[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 == 概要 == 三島市の梅名地区の東部、[[御殿川]]の西脇に鎮座している。 [[式内社]]である'''伊波氐別命神社'''（いわてわけのみことじんじゃ）、あるいは'''阿米都瀬気多知命神社'''（あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ）の論社となっている[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ 右内神社] - Shrine Heritager。 [[左内神社]]と共に三嶋大社の御門の守護神として、下田街道を挟んで左右に祀られ、古くから武家に敬われてきた[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市。 [[源頼朝]]も三嶋大社への途上で度々参詣したといわれ、ある時、手洗い水がないことを不自由に思った頼朝が[[薙刀]]で参道地面を突いたところ、水が湧き出したという「頼朝の手洗い水」の逸話も残っている。 == 祭神 == * [[櫛石窓命]] == 境内 == * [[力石]]（ちから石2つ、女石1つ） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[左内神社]] * [[三嶋大社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁 * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市 ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市 {{デフォルトソート:うないしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 右内神社 |画像 = [[File:Unai-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|display=inline,title}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=右内神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |祭神 = [[櫛石窓命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = }} '''右内神社'''（うないじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名に鎮座する[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 == 概要 == 三島市の梅名地区の東部、[[御殿川]]の西脇に鎮座している。 [[式内社]]である'''伊波氐別命神社'''（いわてわけのみことじんじゃ）、あるいは'''阿米都瀬気多知命神社'''（あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ）の論社となっている[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ 右内神社] - Shrine Heritager。 [[左内神社]]と共に三嶋大社の御門の守護神として、下田街道を挟んで左右に祀られ、古くから武家に敬われてきた[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市。 [[源頼朝]]も三嶋大社への途上で度々参詣したといわれ、ある時、手洗い水がないことを不自由に思った頼朝が[[薙刀]]で参道地面を突いたところ、水が湧き出したという「頼朝の手洗い水」の逸話も残っている。 またかつて社地に宇米津の池（うなぎの池）と呼ばれる池があり、三島地域の中でも特にうなぎを神聖視する地域として知られ、当社は「うなぎの宮」「うなぎの森神社」とも呼ばれ、氏子の間には明治初期までうなぎを食べない習慣があった[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ うなぎ小話] - 三島商工会議所。 == 祭神 == * [[櫛石窓命]] == 境内 == * [[力石]]（ちから石2つ、女石1つ） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[左内神社]] * [[三嶋大社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁 * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市 ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市 {{デフォルトソート:うないしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v9.py-->
{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unai Shrine'''|右内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 4 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 右内神社 |画像 = [[File:Unai-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=右内神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |祭神 = [[櫛石窓命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = }} '''右内神社'''（うないじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名に鎮座する[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 == 概要 == 三島市の梅名地区の東部、[[御殿川]]の西脇に鎮座している。 [[式内社]]である'''伊波氐別命神社'''（いわてわけのみことじんじゃ）、あるいは'''阿米都瀬気多知命神社'''（あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ）の論社となっている[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ 右内神社] - Shrine Heritager。 [[左内神社]]と共に三嶋大社の御門の守護神として、下田街道を挟んで左右に祀られ、古くから武家に敬われてきた[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市。 [[源頼朝]]も三嶋大社への途上で度々参詣したといわれ、ある時、手洗い水がないことを不自由に思った頼朝が[[薙刀]]で参道地面を突いたところ、水が湧き出したという「頼朝の手洗い水」の逸話も残っている。 またかつて社地に宇米津の池（うなぎの池）と呼ばれる池があり、三島地域の中でも特にうなぎを神聖視する地域として知られ、当社は「うなぎの宮」「うなぎの森神社」とも呼ばれ、氏子の間には明治初期までうなぎを食べない習慣があった[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ うなぎ小話] - 三島商工会議所。 == 祭神 == * [[櫛石窓命]] == 境内 == * [[力石]]（ちから石2つ、女石1つ） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[左内神社]] * [[三嶋大社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁 * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市 ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市 {{デフォルトソート:うないしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[右内神社]] to [[Unai Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content

拝殿

page|ja|右内神社|version=103460281|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 右内神社 |画像 = [[File:Unai-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=右内神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |祭神 = [[櫛石窓命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = }} '''右内神社'''（うないじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名に鎮座する[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 == 概要 == 三島市の梅名地区の東部、[[御殿川]]の西脇に鎮座している。 [[式内社]]である'''伊波氐別命神社'''（いわてわけのみことじんじゃ）、あるいは'''阿米都瀬気多知命神社'''（あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ）の論社となっている[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ 右内神社] - Shrine Heritager。 [[左内神社]]と共に三嶋大社の御門の守護神として、下田街道を挟んで左右に祀られ、古くから武家に敬われてきた[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市。 [[源頼朝]]も三嶋大社への途上で度々参詣したといわれ、ある時、手洗い水がないことを不自由に思った頼朝が[[薙刀]]で参道地面を突いたところ、水が湧き出したという「頼朝の手洗い水」の逸話も残っている。 またかつて社地に宇米津の池（うなぎの池）と呼ばれる池があり、三島地域の中でも特にうなぎを神聖視する地域として知られ、当社は「うなぎの宮」「うなぎの森神社」とも呼ばれ、氏子の間には明治初期までうなぎを食べない習慣があった[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ うなぎ小話] - 三島商工会議所。 == 祭神 == * [[櫛石窓命]] == 境内 == * [[力石]]（ちから石2つ、女石1つ） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[左内神社]] * [[三嶋大社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁 * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市 ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市 {{デフォルトソート:うないしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[JA:右内神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134927903}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|右内神社|version=103460281|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 右内神社 |画像 = [[File:Unai-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=右内神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |祭神 = [[櫛石窓命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = }} '''右内神社'''（うないじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[三島市]]梅名に鎮座する[[神社]][http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 == 概要 == 三島市の梅名地区の東部、[[御殿川]]の西脇に鎮座している。 [[式内社]]である'''伊波氐別命神社'''（いわてわけのみことじんじゃ）、あるいは'''阿米都瀬気多知命神社'''（あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ）の論社となっている[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ 右内神社] - Shrine Heritager。 [[左内神社]]と共に三嶋大社の御門の守護神として、下田街道を挟んで左右に祀られ、古くから武家に敬われてきた[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市。 [[源頼朝]]も三嶋大社への途上で度々参詣したといわれ、ある時、手洗い水がないことを不自由に思った頼朝が[[薙刀]]で参道地面を突いたところ、水が湧き出したという「頼朝の手洗い水」の逸話も残っている。 またかつて社地に宇米津の池（うなぎの池）と呼ばれる池があり、三島地域の中でも特にうなぎを神聖視する地域として知られ、当社は「うなぎの宮」「うなぎの森神社」とも呼ばれ、氏子の間には明治初期までうなぎを食べない習慣があった[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ うなぎ小話] - 三島商工会議所。 == 祭神 == * [[櫛石窓命]] == 境内 == * [[力石]]（ちから石2つ、女石1つ） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[左内神社]] * [[三嶋大社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁 * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市 ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市 ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

[[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[JA:右内神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134927903}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|右内神社|version=103460281|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 右内神社 |画像 = [[File:Unai-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|三島市|Q653478}}梅名1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=右内神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |祭神 = {{jalink|櫛石窓命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = }} '''右内神社'''（うないじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|三島市|Q653478}}梅名に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 == 概要 == 三島市の梅名地区の東部、{{jalink|御殿川|Q18988494}}の西脇に鎮座している。 {{jalink|式内社}}である'''伊波氐別命神社'''（いわてわけのみことじんじゃ）、あるいは'''阿米都瀬気多知命神社'''（あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ）の論社となっている[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ 右内神社] - Shrine Heritager。 {{jalink|左内神社|Q134926924}}と共に三嶋大社の御門の守護神として、下田街道を挟んで左右に祀られ、古くから武家に敬われてきた[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市。 {{jalink|源頼朝|Q242800}}も三嶋大社への途上で度々参詣したといわれ、ある時、手洗い水がないことを不自由に思った頼朝が{{jalink|薙刀|Q546116}}で参道地面を突いたところ、水が湧き出したという「頼朝の手洗い水」の逸話も残っている。 またかつて社地に宇米津の池（うなぎの池）と呼ばれる池があり、三島地域の中でも特にうなぎを神聖視する地域として知られ、当社は「うなぎの宮」「うなぎの森神社」とも呼ばれ、氏子の間には明治初期までうなぎを食べない習慣があった[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ うなぎ小話] - 三島商工会議所。 == 祭神 == * {{jalink|櫛石窓命}} == 境内 == * {{jalink|力石|Q1660393}}（ちから石2つ、女石1つ） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * {{jalink|左内神社|Q134926924}} * {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁 * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市 ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市 ==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|三島市|Q653478}}梅名1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=右内神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |祭神 = {{jalink|櫛石窓命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = }} '''右内神社'''（うないじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|三島市|Q653478}}梅名に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁。 === 概要 === 三島市の梅名地区の東部、{{jalink|御殿川|Q18988494}}の西脇に鎮座している。 {{jalink|式内社}}である'''伊波氐別命神社'''（いわてわけのみことじんじゃ）、あるいは'''阿米都瀬気多知命神社'''（あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ）の論社となっている[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ 右内神社] - Shrine Heritager。 {{jalink|左内神社|Q134926924}}と共に三嶋大社の御門の守護神として、下田街道を挟んで左右に祀られ、古くから武家に敬われてきた[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市。 {{jalink|源頼朝|Q242800}}も三嶋大社への途上で度々参詣したといわれ、ある時、手洗い水がないことを不自由に思った頼朝が{{jalink|薙刀|Q546116}}で参道地面を突いたところ、水が湧き出したという「頼朝の手洗い水」の逸話も残っている。 またかつて社地に宇米津の池（うなぎの池）と呼ばれる池があり、三島地域の中でも特にうなぎを神聖視する地域として知られ、当社は「うなぎの宮」「うなぎの森神社」とも呼ばれ、氏子の間には明治初期までうなぎを食べない習慣があった[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ うなぎ小話] - 三島商工会議所。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|櫛石窓命}} === 境内 === * {{jalink|力石|Q1660393}}（ちから石2つ、女石1つ） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|左内神社|Q134926924}} * {{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772}} === 外部リンク === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 右内神社] - 静岡県神社庁 * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html 右内神社] - 三島市 ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm 神社] - 三島市 {{デフォルトソート:うないしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{wikidata link|Q134927903}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Unai Shrine |Image = [[File:Unai-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}} Umena 1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Unai Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative names = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = October 18 |Rituals = }} '''Unai Shrine''' (うないじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Umena, {{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines === Overview === It is situated on the eastern side of the Umena district of Mishima City, on the western bank of the {{jalink|Gotengawa River|Q18988494}}. It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{jalink|Sanai Shrine|Q134926924}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{jalink|naginata|Q546116}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine grounds === * {{jalink|Chikara-ishi|Q1660393}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Sanai Shrine|Q134926924}} * {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City {{DEFAULTSORT:Unai Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{wikidata link|Q134927903}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Unai Shrine |Image = [[File:Unai-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}} Umena 1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Unai Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative names = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = October 18 |Rituals = }} '''Unai Shrine''' (うないじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Umena, {{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines === Overview === It is situated on the eastern side of the Umena district of Mishima City, on the western bank of the {{jalink|Gotengawa River|Q18988494}}. It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{jalink|Sanai Shrine|Q134926924}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{jalink|naginata|Q546116}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine grounds === * {{jalink|Chikara-ishi|Q1660393}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Sanai Shrine|Q134926924}} * {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City {{DEFAULTSORT:Unai Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{jalink|Sanai Shrine|Q134926924}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{jalink|naginata|Q546116}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine grounds === * {{jalink|Chikara-ishi|Q1660393}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Sanai Shrine|Q134926924}} * {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City {{DEFAULTSORT:Unai Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |Location = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}} Umena 1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Unai Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative names = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = October 18 |Rituals = }} '''Unai Shrine''' (うないじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Umena, {{jalink|Mishima City|Q653478}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines === Overview === It is situated on the eastern side of the Umena district of Mishima City, on the western bank of the {{jalink|Gotengawa River|Q18988494}}. It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{jalink|Sanai Shrine|Q134926924}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{jalink|naginata|Q546116}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine grounds === * {{jalink|Chikara-ishi|Q1660393}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|Sanai Shrine|Q134926924}} * {{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City {{DEFAULTSORT:Unai Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}} Umena 1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Unai Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill||lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative names = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = October 18 |Rituals = }} '''Unai Shrine''' (うないじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Umena, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines === Overview === It is situated on the eastern side of the Umena district of Mishima City, on the western bank of the {{ill|lt=Gotengawa River|Q18988494|WD=Q18988494}}. It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{ill|lt=naginata|Q546116|WD=Q546116}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{ill||lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City {{DEFAULTSORT:Unai Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}} Umena 1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Unai Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative names = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = October 18 |Rituals = }} '''Unai Shrine''' (うないじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Umena, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines === Overview === It is situated on the eastern side of the Umena district of Mishima City, on the western bank of the {{ill|lt=Gotengawa River|Q18988494|WD=Q18988494}}. It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{ill|lt=naginata|Q546116|WD=Q546116}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City {{DEFAULTSORT:Unai Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{ill|lt=naginata|Q546116|WD=Q546116}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City {{DEFAULTSORT:Unai Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924|1=Sanai Shrine}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{ill|lt=naginata|Q546116|WD=Q546116|1=Naginata}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924|1=Sanai Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City {{DEFAULTSORT:Unai Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}} Umena 1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Unai Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative names = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = October 18 |Rituals = }} '''Unai Shrine''' (うないじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Umena, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines === Overview === It is situated on the eastern side of the Umena district of Mishima City, on the western bank of the {{ill|lt=Gotengawa River|Q18988494|WD=Q18988494|1=Gotengawa River}}. It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924|1=Sanai Shrine}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{ill|lt=naginata|Q546116|WD=Q546116|1=Naginata}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924|1=Sanai Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]] [[Category:Buildings in Mishima, Shizuoka]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}} Umena 1 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Unai Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|5|39.2|N|138|55|48.7|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative names = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = October 18 |Rituals = }} '''Unai Shrine''' (うないじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Umena, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines === Overview === It is situated on the eastern side of the Umena district of Mishima City, on the western bank of the {{ill|lt=Gotengawa River|Q18988494|WD=Q18988494|1=Gotengawa River}}. It is considered a candidate shrine (ronsha) for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Iwatewake-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわてわけのみことじんじゃ), or alternatively the '''Ametsuseketachi-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (あめつせけたちのみことじんじゃ).[https://shrineheritager.com/unai-shrine/amp/ Unai Shrine] - Shrine Heritager Together with {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924|1=Sanai Shrine}}, it was enshrined on the left and right sides across the Shimoda Highway as guardian deities of the gate of Mishima Taisha, and has long been revered by the warrior class.[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City[https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City It is said that {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|WD=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} often stopped to worship here on his way to Mishima Taisha. According to legend, when Yoritomo found it inconvenient that there was no water for ritual handwashing, he struck the ground of the approach with his {{ill|lt=naginata|Q546116|WD=Q546116|1=Naginata}}, causing water to spring forth—an episode known as “Yoritomo’s Handwashing Water.” In the past, there was a pond on the shrine grounds called Umezu Pond (also known as “Eel Pond”), and the area was known as one that held eels in particular reverence within the Mishima region. The shrine was also called “Eel Shrine” or “Eel Forest Shrine,” and among parishioners there was a custom, lasting until the early Meiji period, of not eating eels.[https://www.mishima-cci.or.jp/796-2/ Eel anecdotes] - Mishima Chamber of Commerce and Industry === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Chikara-ishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} (two “strength stones” and one “women’s stone”) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sanai Shrine|Q134926924|WD=Q134926924|1=Sanai Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402004 Unai Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines * [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/ipn028868.html Unai Shrine] - Mishima City ** [https://www.city.mishima.shizuoka.jp/mishima_info/amenity/rekishi/jinja/jinnja.htm Shrines] - Mishima City == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Mishima, Shizuoka]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]]
